title: bridge-slack: future timestamp bypasses replay guard
description: verify_slack_signature uses now.saturating_sub(ts).saturating_abs() > 300 (slack.rs:111). When ts > now (future timestamp), saturating_sub saturates to 0, so the absolute-value of 0 is 0, which is never > 300. Any request with a timestamp in the future (e.g. year 2099) passes the replay-window check. The HMAC check still catches forged requests, but a legitimate captured request can be replayed by retransmitting with any future timestamp and the original HMAC — the base string uses the original timestamp so the HMAC stays valid. Correct check: (now - ts).abs() > 300, using checked subtraction and abs on the signed result, not saturating_sub.
